Sport|Olympics

Day 12 at theOlympicspromises plenty more excitement and potentialTeam GBmedals in Paris.

Great Britain begin Wednesday positioned fifth in the medal table, behind the United States, China, Australia and hosts France.

The gold count is up to 12 afterrecord-breaking women’s team sprint successto go along withKeely Hodgkinson’s heroicsearlier this week, with 15 silvers and 19 bronzes putting them on a healthy 46 for the Games so far.

The British athletes will be desperate to increase that tally further, with more opportunities to come on the athletics track, inside the cycling Velodrome and elsewhere.

All times BST

From 8am: Women’s golf

The women’s golf action kicks off in Paris this morning following the conclusion of a thrilling men’s competition on Sunday.

It was a dramatic finale at Le Golf National, withScottie Scheffler just pipping Britain’s Tommy Fleetwood to goldafter a nightmare for Jon Rahm.

We should be in for more drama over the next few days, with reigning world No1 Nelly Korda the favourite. GB’s Charley Hull will hope to add an Olympic medal to her two LPGA Tour wins, but admits she could be impacted by a smoking ban on the course.

From 12:52pm - Cycling team pursuit

It’s been a successful start for Team GB in the Velodrome, with a phenomenal gold in the women’s team sprint followed by themen’s sprint team claiming an impressive silveron Tuesday.

Now the respective team pursuiters will be looking to follow suit, with the men’s lineup of Ethan Hayter, Ollie Wood, Charlie Tanfield and Ethan Vernon already guaranteed a medal as they prepare to take on Australia for gold.

The women’s group of Elinor Barker, Josie Knight, Anna Morris and Jess Roberts will also be looking to book their place in the gold medal showdown as they face the USA.

8:20pm - Men’s 400m final

We’ve already seen one odds-on British track favourite live up to their lofty billing this week in Hodgkinson - can Matt Hudson-Smith now do the same?

Aquatics GB 'immensely disappointed' as Proud joins Enhanced Games

Keely Hodgkinson reveals Grand Slam Track decision and 2025 ambitions

Make 2026 the year of working smarter

He has cruised into the men’s 400m final, with the Wolverhampton star now looking to add an Olympic medal to his 2023 World Championship silver.

TV channel:The BBC is broadcasting two live streams of the action for the duration of the Games. This will be on BBC One, BBC Two or a stream available by clicking the red button on Freeview or a smart TV.

On Wednesday, it’s 8am BST until 1pm on BBC One and again from 1:45pm until 6pm, plus 7pm until 10pm. BBC Two briefly pick up the coverage between 1pm and 1.45pm, and 6pm and 7pm.

There is reduced coverage compared with previous years, when the BBC broadcasted every event on its website or channels. Instead, Discovery+ is the main rights holder forParis 2024and is showing every moment across the Eurosport channels in the UK from 7am to 10.30pm every day.

Live stream:Fans can also catch the BBC coverage live online for free via the BBC Sport website and BBC iPlayer. The Discovery+ app will be showing extensive coverage, which begins at a monthly fee of £3.99.

Highlights:The BBC are showing a nightly highlights show called Tonight at the Games, which on Wednesday night is being shown on BBC One at 10.40pm.

Live blog:You can follow all the action on Day 12 at the Olympics viaStandard Sport’srolling live blog, with expert analysis from reporters Matt Majendie and Malik Ouzia out in Paris.

title: Today's Olympics schedule: Team GB events, Day 12 medal highlights and how to watch the Paris Games | The Standard
author: Jonathan Gorrie, George Flood
description: The athletics and cycling tracks could yield more British success on Wednesday
